questions.question: Define an operating system and enumerate its essential functions.
keyPoints: Definition: software that manages hardware resources and provides services to user programs
Core functions: process management, memory management, file system, I/O device handling
Additional services: security, networking, user interface
Role as resource manager: mediates competing requests from processes

questions.question: Describe the boot process of a computer system up to the loading of the OS kernel, covering BIOS/UEFI, boot loader, and kernel initialization.
keyPoints: BIOS/UEFI reads the Master Boot Record (MBR) or EFI partition to locate the boot loader
Boot loader (e.g., GRUB) loads the OS kernel into memory and may present a menu
Kernel initializes core subsystems (memory manager, scheduler, drivers) and spawns the init (systemd) process

questions.question: Compare the monolithic, microkernel, and layered structures of operating systems. Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of each and give an example of an OS that implements each.
keyPoints: Monolithic: all OS services run in kernel space; simple, fast, but difficult to maintain (e.g., Linux, classic Unix)
Microkernel: minimal kernel; services run in user space; better reliability, extensibility, but higher IPC overhead (e.g., MINIX3, GNU Hurd)
Layered: OS built in hierarchical layers; each layer provides services to the layer above (e.g., THE, early Windows NT)
Trade‑offs: monolithic offers performance but less modularity; microkernel offers safety at cost of performance; layered offers clear design but can be complex

questions.question: Explain what a system call is, describe the typical sequence of a system call invoked from a user program, and categorize the major types of system calls with examples.
keyPoints: System call: programming interface for user programs to request services from the kernel (privileged mode)

Typical sequence: user program calls library wrapper → wrapper places syscall number and arguments in registers → issues trap/interrupt → CPU switches to kernel mode → kernel executes handler → returns result to user space

Major categories: process control (fork, exec, exit), file management (open, read, write, close), device management (ioctl, read/write on devices), information maintenance (getpid, getuid), communication (pipe, socket, msgget), protection (chmod, chown)
System calls enable controlled access to hardware and ensure protection and isolation of user processes
